dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7186 IH: American Family Housing Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Miller of Illinois (for herself and Ms. Boebert) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Financial Services

form.official-title: To amend the Investment Company Act of 1940 to prohibit certain large-scale companies from purchasing single family residences.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the American Family Housing Act.

section.header: Restrictions on purchasing single family residences
section.text: The Investment Company Act of 1940 (15 U.S.C. 80a–1 et seq.) is amended by inserting after section 12 the following:

section.header: Restrictions on purchasing single family residences

subsection.text: Beginning on the date that is 100 days after the date of the enactment of this section, a large-scale company may not—

paragraph.text: purchase (including from the Federal Government) any single family residence; or
purchase the equity securities of a qualified issuer if such purchase would result in holding more than 49 percent of the equity securities of such qualified issuer.
The term large-scale company means a registered investment company or private fund with more than $100,000,000,000 in assets under management.
The term private fund means an issuer that would be an investment company, but for paragraph (1) or (7) of section 3(c).
The term qualified issuer means an issuer that holds, as assets, more than 100 single family residences.

clause.text: consisting of 1 dwelling unit built for one household to live in; and
that functions as an independent living space without shared walls or shared utilities; and

subparagraph.text: does not include a dwelling unit in a condominium or cooperative housing project.
